tagline: Open a merge request, then forget it — a background watchdog drives its CI to green, fixing failures at the root.
summary: Triggered by an open merge request (not a manual command), mr-watchdog runs a detached background watcher over the MR's remote CI. While you move on, it polls the pipeline; on a red check it pulls the failing job log, spawns a headless agent to fix the ROOT CAUSE, commits the fix on the branch to re-trigger CI, and loops until green — then tells you 'ok c'est bon'. It refuses to fake green: no skipped/deleted tests, no --no-verify, no || true, no continue-on-error, no lowered thresholds; if the only way out is a workaround it stops and hands back to you. Never merges, never touches the default branch, never force-pushes. Bounded fix attempts. The natural sequel to ship-when-done.
capabilities: MR-triggered, not manual: a detached watcher starts when an open MR with live CI is detected, and survives across turns
Root-cause fixes only — a curated anti-bypass gate reverts any 'fix' that skips/deletes tests or disables a check
Forge-agnostic CI: GitHub via gh, GitLab via glab — polls status, fetches the failing job log
Hard guardrails: never merges, never commits/pushes the default branch, never force-pushes, bounded fix attempts
Opt-in per repo; surfaces 'green / blocked / exhausted' back in your session and optionally as a desktop notification
